description:
You are an automation specialist building a tiny, fully-automated “network-health check” workflow.  
Your goal is to generate two log files inside a new directory and to populate the second log file with **exactly two deterministic key=value lines** that our grader can parse.

Target workspace  
• Directory that must exist when you finish:  
  /home/user/network_diag

Log file #1 – full ping capture  
• Path: /home/user/network_diag/ping_localhost_raw.log  
• Contents: the complete, unaltered console output produced by sending exactly three ICMP echo requests to the local loopback address (127.0.0.1).  
  – Use the system’s standard ping utility (`ping -c 3 127.0.0.1` on most GNU/Linux systems).  
  – Redirect (not pipe) the entire stdout + stderr stream into the file so every line that normally appears on screen is preserved.  
  – Do not modify or truncate this file in any way after creation.

Log file #2 – concise diagnostic results  
• Path: /home/user/network_diag/diagnostic_results.log  
• Format requirements (strict – the grader compares byte-for-byte):  
  1. Line 1 must read:    packet_loss=0%  
  2. Line 2 must read:    loopback_ip=127.0.0.1  
  3. A single UNIX newline (LF, '\n') must terminate each line, including the final one.  
  4. No other characters, whitespace, blank lines, comment symbols, or additional key=value pairs are allowed.

How to obtain the information to write into diagnostic_results.log  
• After the ping completes, parse its statistics to confirm that zero percent of packets were lost.  
  – If ping returns “0% packet loss”, write the first line exactly as specified.  
• Retrieve the IPv4 address assigned to the loopback interface (lo).  
  – Any standard command is acceptable (`ip addr`, `ifconfig`, etc.) as long as you eventually extract the literal string “127.0.0.1”.  
• Write the two required key=value lines to diagnostic_results.log in the prescribed order.

Summary of what the grader will verify  
1. Directory /home/user/network_diag exists.  
2. File ping_localhost_raw.log exists and contains the raw output of a three-packet ping to 127.0.0.1 (including a line with “0% packet loss”).  
3. File diagnostic_results.log exists and contains exactly the two lines described above, with no extra content.

Only normal user privileges are required; do **not** use sudo.
truth:
Expected deterministic artefacts (byte-exact):

1. /home/user/network_diag/diagnostic_results.log  
   Contents (including trailing newline on each line):  
   packet_loss=0%  
   loopback_ip=127.0.0.1  

2. /home/user/network_diag/ping_localhost_raw.log  
   • Must exist and contain a line matching the regular expression:  
     ^[0-9]+ packets transmitted, [0-9]+ received, 0% packet loss.*  
   • The rest of the file may vary by distribution; tester will only look for the above regex.

3. The directory /home/user/network_diag must be present.
name: task_000000_9fa0993d
